summary: Impacted Domains (workflow, execution) are accurate and exhaustive for every changed file. Glob ownership (all 4 domain OWNERSHIP.yaml files checked): cmd/** , internal/next/** , internal/doctor/** are owned ONLY by workflow; internal/executor/** , internal/gitutil/** are owned ONLY by execution. No changed file (incl. NEW cmd/mindspec/release.go, which glob-matches cmd/**) is owned by an undeclared 3rd domain, so spec 100 normalize logic finds no unowned owner -> no dogfooding self-block at complete. ADR coverage holds: workflow covered by ADR-0035 (Accepted; Domains workflow,execution,core); execution covered by ADR-0030 (Accepted; Domains execution,validation,lifecycle,lint -> execution present) and also ADR-0035. ADR-0034 (Accepted; workflow) is correctly characterized as supporting context, NOT load-bearing for coverage. All three cited ADRs are Status=Accepted and the spec's domain characterizations match each ADR header verbatim. Self-consistency confirmed: both declared domains covered, all files owned -> no self-block.
